visual_checks: Look for a 'Skip to main content' or 'Skip navigation' link at the very top of the page — it may be visually hidden until it receives keyboard focus
Check if the page has repeated navigation blocks (header, sidebar nav, footer links) that appear on every page of the site
Look for ARIA landmark roles (banner, navigation, main, contentinfo) or HTML5 landmark elements (header, nav, main, footer) that allow assistive technology users to jump between sections
Check if heading elements (h1-h6) are used to demarcate sections, allowing screen reader users to navigate by headings
Verify that any skip link target actually moves keyboard focus to the main content area when activated
pass_conditions: A functioning skip navigation link is present that moves focus past the repeated navigation to the main content area
ARIA landmark roles or HTML5 landmark elements are used to identify page regions (navigation, main, complementary, contentinfo)
Headings are used to organize content sections, enabling heading-based navigation in screen readers
Skip links become visible when they receive keyboard focus (if visually hidden by default)
Multiple mechanisms exist for bypassing repeated content (skip links, landmarks, headings)
fail_conditions: No skip navigation mechanism is provided and users must Tab through the entire navigation on every page
A skip link exists but does not function — focus does not move to the target or the link is broken
No ARIA landmarks or HTML5 landmark elements are used to identify page regions
The page has extensive repeated content blocks with no headings or landmarks to allow users to jump past them
Skip link is present but permanently hidden (display:none or visibility:hidden) so it is inaccessible to both sighted keyboard users and screen readers
na_conditions: Page has no repeated blocks of content (e.g., a single-page application with no persistent navigation)
Page is a standalone page with no content that repeats across multiple pages
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Cannot verify skip link functionality from static screenshots — keyboard interaction testing required
Page DOM could not be captured to verify landmark structure
common_mistakes: Skip link exists in the HTML but is permanently hidden with display:none, making it unusable
Skip link target ID does not match an element on the page, so activating it does nothing
Skip link jumps to the wrong location (e.g., to a sidebar instead of main content)
Using only ARIA landmarks without a visible skip link — sighted keyboard users cannot use landmarks without a screen reader
Multiple nav elements on the page with no aria-label to distinguish them (e.g., 'Main navigation' vs 'Footer navigation')
Skip link is only visible on hover, not on keyboard focus

examples.pass: The page has a 'Skip to main content' link that becomes visible on keyboard focus, the main content is wrapped in <main>, navigation is in <nav aria-label='Primary'>, and headings break the content into navigable sections
examples.fail: The page has a complex header with 45 navigation links and no skip link, no landmark roles, and no heading hierarchy — keyboard users must Tab through all 45 links on every page visit
examples.partial: A skip link is present and functional but the page has two <nav> regions with no aria-label to distinguish them, and the footer content area has no landmark

plain_meaning: Users must have a way to skip past blocks of content that repeat on many pages (header, navigation, sidebars) to get directly to the main content. The intent is that keyboard and screen-reader users are not forced to wade through dozens of repeated links on every page.
scope_applies_to: Pages that share a common header, navigation, or sidebar with other pages
Templates with repeated content blocks before the main content
Any repeated block that would force keyboard users to tab through it on every page
scope_does_not_apply_to: Single-page applications with no repeated blocks
Pages that are unique with no shared navigation
Focus order within the main content (that is SC 2.4.3)
Heading quality or descriptiveness (that is SC 2.4.6)
borderline_calibration: ANY ONE of the following mechanisms satisfies the SC: a skip link, ARIA landmarks (banner/nav/main/etc.), properly structured headings allowing jump navigation, or frames/iframes with titles. You do not need all of them.
Do not fail a page for missing a skip link if it has a main landmark and a proper heading structure.
A skip link that is hidden until focused is still a valid mechanism.
If the page has no repeated blocks, this SC is N/A.
